content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges provided, we can scan each edge and collect the time t whenever node 2 and node 7 are connected.

We start by initializing two sets, one for node 0 and one for node 2:
```css
set_0 = {0}
set_2 = {2}
```
We then iterate over all edges in the graph:
```css
for edge in graph:
    u, v, t = edge
    if u in set_0 and v in set_2:
        set_0.remove(u)
        set_2.remove(v)
        set_0.add(v)
        set_2.add(u)
        t = max(t, t2)
        t2 = t
```
We repeat this process for node 2 and node 7:
```css
for edge in graph:
    u, v, t = edge
    if u in set_2 and v in set_7:
        set_2.remove(u)
        set_7.remove(v)
        set_2.add(v)
        set_7.add(u)
        t = max(t, t2)
        t2 = t
```
After iterating over all edges, we can find the earliest time when node 2 and node 7 are connected. In this case, it is at time 2.

Therefore, the answer is:

Answer: 2
